alt_forms.note: Native Japanese equivalent. Recommended in miniature wargames to avoid exact compounds used in WH Japan rulebooks.
Alternative native-Japanese phase-word; carries nuance of a turning point or critical stage.

definitions.gloss_en: a defined subdivision of a turn or round during which specific actions occur
definitions.gloss_jp: {手|て}{番|ばん}や{ラウンド|らうんど}を{分|わ}けた{段|だん}{階|かい}；{特|とく}{定|てい}の{行|こう}{動|どう}が{許|ゆる}される{時|じ}{間|かん}の{区|く}{切|き}り
definitions.explanation_en: A structural subdivision of play time. Each phase constrains which actions are legal. Phases execute sequentially within a turn.
definitions.implicit_notes: フェイズ alone is low-risk as IP (it is simply the English loanword 'phase'). The risk arises with specific compounds: 突撃フェイズ, 射撃フェイズ, 白兵戦フェイズ, 移動フェイズ are the exact named phase terms used in WH40K Japan and AoS Japan rulebooks.
definitions.orthography_notes: Katakana loanword from English 'phase'. フェイズ alone is used independently in Fire Emblem Japan (自軍フェイズ, 敵軍フェイズ) and Famicom Wars (発動フェイズ), predating WH Japan usage. 段階 (native compound: stage/step) is the recommended neutral alternative for phase-type compounds in this wargame project.
definitions.game_setting_notes: In video games (SRPGs): 自軍フェイズ (player phase) / 敵軍フェイズ (enemy phase) is the Fire Emblem standard. In miniature wargames: using 段階 instead of フェイズ in phase-type compounds avoids WH Japan terminology.

usage_jp: {自|じ}{軍|ぐん}フェイズ (player phase, Fire Emblem); {突|とつ}{撃|げき}{段|だん}{階|かい} (charging phase, recommended wargame form)
comparative_notes: 段階 (dankai) is the recommended substitute in miniature wargame compounds. 局面 (kyokumen) implies a broader situational shift and is less common as a phase-label word. See individual phase entries (00008–00011) for each phase type.

examples.japanese: {自|じ}{軍|ぐん}フェイズに{移|い}{動|どう}する。

cross_references.note: 突撃段階 — charging phase (段階 compound)
移動段階 — moving phase (段階 compound)
射撃段階 — shooting phase (段階 compound)
白兵戦段階 — fighting phase (段階 compound)
